description:
Doom Emacs development has slowed (3.0 release in limbo, less active maintenance).
The config doesn't use Evil (Doom's biggest value-add), and most customization
is already in a private module. Nix already handles package management, making
straight.el redundant.

## Why migrate

- Not using Evil — removes Doom's biggest value proposition
- Most P1 modernization recommendations point to Emacs built-ins (corfu, eglot, flymake)
- Nix-managed packages are more reproducible than straight.el
- Faster startup without Doom's framework overhead
- Emacs 29+ absorbed many formerly-external packages (use-package, eglot, flymake, tree-sitter, project.el)

## Lazy loading strategy

The main reason for staying with Doom was its lazy loading framework. Replacements:
- **use-package** (built-in since Emacs 29): covers 90% of Doom's `after!`/`use-package!`
  via `:defer`, `:commands`, `:hook`, `:mode`, `:after`
- Doom's `after!` is just `with-eval-after-load`
- Doom's `add-hook!` is syntactic sugar over `add-hook`
- Nix handles package installation, so only need use-package for config + defer

## Migration path (incremental)

1. Set up `home-manager`'s `programs.emacs` with emacs-overlay for Nix-managed packages
2. Port the private module config (org-roam, notmuch, bibtex, windmove, etc.)
   into standalone `use-package` declarations
3. Replace Doom modules one-by-one with native equivalents:
   - company → corfu (built-in compatible)
   - lsp → eglot (built-in)
   - syntax → flymake (built-in)
   - vertico stack stays as-is (already native)
4. Drop the Doom framework once all modules are ported

## What to preserve

- org-roam + vulpea + bibtex integration (complex, port carefully)
- notmuch email config
- org-super-agenda custom views
- WezTerm/tmux smart pane navigation
- org-capture protocol frame handling
- g3doc exporter (if still needed — see nix-yx8.12)
- org-yank-dwim functionality

## Scope

This is a large task. Consider doing it as a parallel config that can be
tested alongside Doom before cutting over. The Nix setup makes this
feasible — both configs can coexist.
title: Explore migration from Doom Emacs to vanilla Emacs + Nix
